my_thoughts: My intent says 'Shift their frame...' but the student is in ambivalent_realization. Am I trying to shift their frame or are they already shifting? The comparison_contrast mechanism suggests I'm doing something, but maybe they're doing it to themselves and I'm just naming it.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student says 'I have been through all that yet I am completely dissatisfied.  I have been thro'—ambivalent realization via skeptical concession. Teacher responds '' using comparison contrast to offers diagnosis, shifts locus inward, challenges claim authenticity, defines true discontent. Opening REMAKING move addresses ambivalent realization. Predicting go along compliantly because comparison contrast interventions typically produce this response.
visible_response:
You may be dissatisfied with outward relationships, but perhaps you are seeking some psychological attachment that will give full satisfaction.

I wonder if you really are?

If you were wholly discontented, there would be no movement in any particular direction, would there?

If you are thoroughly dissatisfied with being in a room, you do not seek a bigger room with nicer furniture; yet this desire to find a better room is what you call dissatisfaction.

Your dissatisfaction arises from not having found complete satisfaction.

So you are really not dissatisfied; it is simply that you have not so far been able to find complete and lasting satisfaction in anything.

What you really want is lasting self-gratification.
